perturbation.original_text: Prolong shall use its sponsorship of the Sabco entries for advertising, promoting and marketing itself.
perturbation.changed_text: Prolong may use its sponsorship of the Sabco entries for advertising, promoting and marketing itself.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' introduces ambiguity regarding Prolong's obligation to actively promote its sponsorship. The original text mandates Prolong to use the sponsorship for promotional activities. The altered version suggests that Prolong has the option, but not the obligation, to use the sponsorship for promotional purposes. This creates an in-text contradiction by weakening the initially established promotional requirements, thus altering the expected investment return for Sabco.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Prolong desires to become a major associate sponsor of all three Sabco entries during the 1998, 1999 and year-2000 NASCAR Winston Cup Series racing seasons. These entries shall mean the Sabco No. 40, all NASCAR Winston Cup Series races during this three-year period; the Sabco No. 42, all NASCAR Winston Cup Series races during this three-year period; and Sabco No. 46, all NASCAR Winston Cup Series races during this three-year period. Prolong may use its sponsorship of the Sabco entries for advertising, promoting and marketing itself. WHEREAS, the parties desire to set forth in this Agreement their respective rights and obligations;
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'shall,' mandating Prolong's use of sponsorship for promotional activities, aligning with Sabco's expectation of investment return. The change to 'may' weakens this obligation, creating ambiguity and potentially reducing Prolong's promotional efforts. This directly contradicts the preamble stating 'WHEREAS, the parties desire to set forth in this Agreement their respective rights and obligations;' because the alteration changes Prolong's obligation. The change from 'shall' to 'may' creates a strong contradiction in the level of commitment required of Prolong regarding promotional activity, directly impacting the anticipated benefits for Sabco.

perturbation.original_text: Sabco agrees to provide at no expense to Prolong for its use -------- in promoting its sponsorship, five (5) free show car appearance days whereas Prolong can deem which of the Sabco show cars to use. This shall mean that Prolong may schedule any combination of Sabco's three show cars (based on availability) not to exceed a total of five show days (travel days are considered a "show date"). Sabco will provide transportation of show car and will provide an attendant to stay with the car during its display period.
perturbation.changed_text: Sabco endeavors to provide at no expense to Prolong for its use -------- in promoting its sponsorship, five (5) free show car appearance days whereas Prolong can deem which of the Sabco show cars to use. This shall mean that Prolong may schedule any combination of Sabco's three show cars (based on availability) not to exceed a total of five show days (travel days are considered a "show date"). Sabco will provide transportation of show car and will provide an attendant to stay with the car during its display period.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees' to 'endeavors' weakens Sabco's commitment to provide the show cars. 'Agrees' implies a firm obligation, while 'endeavors' suggests an effort but not a guarantee. This creates uncertainty around Sabco's actual obligation, potentially leading to disputes if the show cars are not consistently available. The altered wording contradicts the initially stated commitment, affecting the reliability of this promotional asset for Prolong.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Show Car. Sabco endeavors to provide at no expense to Prolong for its use -------- in promoting its sponsorship, five (5) free show car appearance days whereas Prolong can deem which of the Sabco show cars to use. This shall mean that Prolong may schedule any combination of Sabco's three show cars (based on availability) not to exceed a total of five show days (travel days are considered a "show date"). Sabco will provide transportation of show car and will provide an attendant to stay with the car during its display period. For any show car appearance requested by Prolong over and above the five (5) free appearances, Sabco will make available unto Prolong a show car at a discounted rate of [ * ] Dollars ($ [ * ]) per day. All travel days to and from the site of a show car appearance, as well as the actual day upon which show car is on display will be charged as a "show date". It is further understood that the parties will agree in advance upon a schedule of places and events for the show car to be displayed at such times as will not interfere with Sabco's racing schedule.
perturbation.justification: While 'agrees' implies a stronger commitment than 'endeavors', both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text use 'endeavors'. Since the contradiction explanation revolves around a change that has already occurred in both sections, there is no contradiction between the two sections, as they both now contain the weakened commitment. Therefore, no meaningful contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, shall allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, should allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'should' weakens the mandatory nature of driver endorsements. 'Shall' establishes a clear obligation for the drivers to allow their likeness to be used, while 'should' suggests a recommendation or expectation, but not a requirement. This creates uncertainty regarding the extent to which Prolong can rely on driver endorsements for marketing purposes. This weakens the value of the sponsorship agreement for Prolong, due to drivers obligations being altered, from must being done, to a recomendation to be done.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Driver Appearances. Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers shall be at the discounted rate of [ * ] ($[ * ]) Dollars per day per driver, [ * ]. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, should allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that drivers 'shall' allow their likeness to be used for endorsements, creating a binding obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'should,' implying a recommendation rather than a requirement. This directly contradicts the section specifying 'Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate driver appearances,' because it creates uncertainty about whether drivers are actually obligated to participate in those appearances or other endorsements. The change undermines Prolong's ability to rely on driver participation as previously agreed, creating an in-text contradiction.
